content.text: How does the router work in this project?

The router uses Express.js routing. Routes are organized in separate files under src/routes/ and mounted in the main app.js. Each route file exports a Router instance with GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE handlers. The main app mounts them with app.use('/api/users', usersRouter) pattern.

payload.message: Summary of session so far: The project is a Node.js Express app with modular routing under src/routes/. Routes handle CRUD operations for users and products. Authentication uses JWT tokens. The database layer uses connection pooling with pg-pool for PostgreSQL.

content.text: Explain the database connection pooling setup

The database connection pooling is configured in src/db/pool.js. It uses pg-pool with these settings: max 20 connections, idle timeout of 30 seconds, and connection timeout of 5 seconds. The pool is created as a singleton and exported for use across the application. Each request gets a connection from the pool and releases it when done.
